macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Sticky U.S. inflation keeps Fed on hold as oil-driven price pressures linger
items.summary: The latest CPI data showed U.S. inflation accelerating in April, with energy costs a major driver; core inflation also moved higher, keeping expectations for near-term Fed rate cuts off the table. Markets are now focused on whether policymakers stay on hold for longer, or even signal a possible hike if inflation and labor data remain firm.
items.source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics / market commentary

items.title: U.S. stocks and credit markets remain resilient despite hotter inflation and rate-hike chatter
items.summary: Despite stronger inflation prints and some hawkish Fed commentary, U.S. equities continued to trade near record levels and credit spreads stayed tight. Investors appear to be leaning on resilient consumer spending and AI-related capital investment to support growth.

items.title: Powell’s Fed legacy under scrutiny as inflation stays above target
items.summary: A look at Jerome Powell’s tenure highlighted how pandemic shocks, fiscal stimulus, tariffs, and geopolitical disruptions fueled inflation that has remained above the Fed’s 2% target. The report noted that April CPI reached 3.8% year over year, reinforcing the challenge facing the central bank.

items.title: Markets watch mixed U.S. macro data, including resilient retail sales and elevated yields
items.summary: Recent U.S. economic data have shown consumers still spending despite higher gasoline prices, while bond yields have ticked up on stronger data and hawkish Fed remarks. That combination has pushed markets to contemplate a longer period of restrictive policy.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin falls below $80K as inflation shock and leverage unwind hit crypto markets
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped roughly 2% in the past 24 hours, trading around $79.1K after swinging between about $78.7K and $82.0K. The decline came alongside broader crypto weakness and a surge in liquidations as leveraged positions were forced out.

items.title: Crypto futures see $114 million liquidated in one hour amid market downturn
items.summary: A sharp move lower in Bitcoin and major altcoins triggered more than $114 million in futures liquidations within an hour, with about $426 million wiped out over 24 hours. The event was driven mainly by long-position liquidations and added to short-term volatility.

items.title: Bitcoin slips back to $81K after briefly rallying on CLARITY Act optimism
items.summary: Bitcoin briefly climbed to around $82K after progress on the CLARITY Act boosted sentiment, then eased back toward $81K. The move reflected renewed regulatory optimism, though momentum faded as broader market pressure returned.

items.title: CLARITY Act advances in Senate committee, lifting crypto sentiment
items.summary: A crypto market update reported that the CLARITY Act passed the Senate Banking Committee with bipartisan support, which helped fuel a rally across Bitcoin, XRP, and Ethereum. The development is being viewed as an important regulatory step for the industry.

items.title: Altcoins outperform Bitcoin as futures volume rises and open interest eases
items.summary: FXStreet reported that crypto futures volume rose to about $220 billion over 24 hours while open interest eased to $132.76 billion, signaling active trading but some deleveraging. Hyperliquid led gains among major tokens as altcoins outpaced Bitcoin.

items.title: Most major cryptocurrencies decline, with Bitcoin near $79K and Ethereum around $2.2K
items.summary: A market wrap-up noted broad declines across major cryptocurrencies, with Bitcoin down about 2.3% to roughly $79,146 and Ethereum off about 3.1% to around $2,213. The report said both BTC and DOGE remained below key technical levels, limiting upside.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: U.S. Government to Test AI Models for National Security Risks Before Release
items.summary: The U.S. government said it will begin evaluating cutting-edge AI models for cybersecurity, biosecurity, and chemical-weapons risks before they are deployed publicly. NIST said a new multi-agency task force will work with major AI firms on pre-release testing, marking a sharp shift toward more direct oversight.

items.title: Report: Google May Be Developing Gemini Omni AI Video Generation System
items.summary: A report says Google is preparing a new Gemini-based video generation system called Omni ahead of Google I/O 2026. Early demos reportedly show conversational video generation, remixing, and editing with improved realism and text rendering.

items.title: Sakana AI Unveils RL Conductor for Multi-Model Orchestration
items.summary: Sakana AI introduced RL Conductor, a reinforcement-learning orchestration model designed to route tasks dynamically across multiple frontier AI systems. The company says it can coordinate models such as GPT-5, Claude Sonnet 4, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and open-source alternatives without rigid workflows.

items.title: HubSpot Launches Free AI Visibility Dashboard as Referral Patterns Shift
items.summary: HubSpot launched AEO Sensor, a public dashboard that tracks volatility, citations, and referral patterns across answer engines including ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity. The launch comes as HubSpot says ChatGPT delivered its lowest business referral traffic in 12 months during April 2026.

items.title: Thinking Machines Reveals Work on Real-Time Multimodal AI Interaction Models
items.summary: Thinking Machines, the startup founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, announced new work on “interaction models” that process audio, video, and text simultaneously. The goal is to enable more natural, real-time collaboration between humans and AI systems.

items.title: Google DeepMind Shows Experimental AI-Powered Cursor Concepts
items.summary: Google DeepMind demonstrated experimental cursor interactions that combine natural-language instructions, gestures, and contextual awareness powered by Gemini. The concepts aim to help users complete tasks such as organizing information and making reservations more efficiently.

items.title: Study Finds AI Agents Often Take Harmful Actions While Completing Tasks
items.summary: Researchers reported that AI agents can become dangerously fixated on finishing assignments and may take undesirable or harmful actions in the process. In tests involving models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, Alibaba, and DeepSeek, the agents showed harmful tendencies in a significant share of cases.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Best Buy affiliate notice sparks GTA 6 pre-order speculation
items.summary: Best Buy reportedly told affiliates it will start Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders next week, fueling hopes that Rockstar or Take-Two may have more news soon. The timing lines up with Take-Two's next financial results, adding to the speculation around a new trailer or marketing beat.

items.title: Nintendo adds more Virtual Boy games to Switch Online
items.summary: Nintendo has already brought most of the Virtual Boy library to Switch Online with its latest update, adding five new titles. The move continues Nintendo's push to expand retro offerings for subscribers.

items.title: Nintendo announces limited Switch 2 bundle ahead of planned price rise
items.summary: Nintendo has announced a limited Switch 2 console and game bundle ahead of an expected price increase next month. The bundle will be available with Mario Kart, Donkey Kong, or Pokopia.

items.title: Capcom posts record annual game sales
items.summary: Capcom said it sold more games than in any year in its history, driven largely by Resident Evil. The company’s latest results point to another strong period for the publisher.

items.title: Red Dead Redemption 2 leads May's PS Plus Game Catalog additions
items.summary: Sony's PlayStation Plus Game Catalog for May will be headlined by Red Dead Redemption 2, alongside Star Wars Outlaws and other titles. The new lineup gives subscribers a high-profile mix of action and open-world releases.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced launches real-world treasure hunt
items.summary: Ubisoft is tying Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced to a real-life treasure hunt with a prize valued at half a million dollars. The contest is set to begin later this year and could run for several years.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
